title: Add monotone-constrained booster on physical pit-window features
summary: Pit probability should usually rise, not fall, as tyre age pressure, finish-tyre deficit, or pace-loss pressure worsen.
rationale: Pit probability should usually rise, not fall, as tyre age pressure, finish-tyre deficit, or pace-loss pressure worsen. Monotone constraints are a good adjacent-competition trick for tabular problems with known directional structure, helping reduce spurious non-causal reversals.
implementation_hint: Train a LightGBM monotone model using only features with credible signs, such as tyre_age_vs_expected, finish_tyre_deficit, lap_delta_loss, old_tyre_late_race_pressure, past_pit_count, and maybe negative monotonicity on laps_remaining when appropriate. Use it as a low-variance specialist and blend rather than forcing all features into the constrained model.
expected_effect: Small standalone gain or stronger ensemble robustness, especially on rare strategy regimes where unconstrained trees overfit noise.
risk: Wrong sign assumptions can hurt AUC; many race features are non-monotone globally, so constrain only a compact trusted subset.
